success_surface: Formula Construction and Multi-Cell Application in Spreadsheet Tasks
effective_behaviors: Use openpyxl directly for formula manipulation when formulas must be preserved
Dynamically construct formula strings with variable row/column references
Apply formulas iteratively across target cell ranges rather than single-cell operations
Verify formula content after assignment by reading back cell values
Prefer direct cell.value assignment over pandas.to_excel for formula retention
evidence: Trajectory used openpyxl.load_workbook() instead of pandas.read_excel() to preserve formula capability
Formula string was built dynamically: '=IF(H' + str(row) + '=...)'
Looped through range(2, 5) to apply formula to multiple target rows (J2:J4)
Verified success by printing ws.cell(row=row, column=10).value after save
Set ws.cell(...).value = formula string starting with '=' for Excel formula recognition

memory_items.title: OpenPyXL for Formula Retention
memory_items.lesson: When task requires inserting or modifying Excel formulas, use openpyxl exclusively; pandas read_excel/to_excel will convert formulas to static values.
memory_items.generalization: Tool selection based on formula preservation requirement overrides data analysis convenience.

memory_items.title: Dynamic Formula String Construction
memory_items.lesson: Build Excel formula strings programmatically using string concatenation with loop variables for row/column references.
memory_items.generalization: Parameterize cell references in formulas rather than hardcoding addresses for multi-cell applications.

memory_items.title: Iterative Multi-Cell Formula Application
memory_items.lesson: For tasks requiring formulas in adjacent or related cells, iterate across the target range and update each cell individually.
memory_items.generalization: Scope awareness drives iteration strategy for consistent formula deployment across ranges.

memory_items.title: Post-Assignment Formula Verification
memory_items.lesson: After writing formulas, read back the cell value to confirm the formula string was correctly assigned before considering task complete.
memory_items.generalization: Verification step ensures formula integrity before file closure or downstream processing.

memory_items.title: Excel Formula Syntax Standardization
memory_items.lesson: Formulas assigned via openpyxl must start with '=' and follow standard Excel notation including proper quoting of text values.
memory_items.generalization: All formula assignments require explicit '=' prefix regardless of how they're constructed programmatically.

edits.content:
## Formula Manipulation Patterns

### Formula Tool Selection
- Always prefer openpyxl over pandas when inserting or modifying Excel formulas. pandas.converts formulas to static values during both read and write operations.
- Use `worksheet.cell().value = '=<formula_string>'` to assign formulas directly.

### Dynamic Formula Construction
- Build formula strings using Python string concatenation to parameterize cell references (row/column numbers).
- Include the '=' prefix explicitly in all formula strings written to cells.
- For nested logic (IF statements), validate formula syntax before assignment.

### Multi-Cell Formula Deployment
- When formulas are needed across multiple rows/columns, iterate over the target range using loops.
- Apply the same formula pattern with adjusted cell references for each target cell.

### Formula Verification
- After writing formulas, verify by reading back the cell value to confirm formula string assignment.
- Print diagnostic output showing the actual formula stored in cells before closing workbooks.
